assertions.text: Vehicle 1 was heading south in lane two of a two-lane protected turn lane that was about to merge into a south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 2 was traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane, two-way road.
Vehicle 2 turned right to travel south into lane two of the south-bound three-lane one-way road.
Vehicle 1 made a right turn across a painted gore before the merge intending to enter a parking lot.
Vehicle 1's right side and Vehicle 2's front end made contact with each other.
There was one impact between Vehicle 1 and Vehicle 2.
Both vehicles came to rest on the road after the collision.
Vehicle 2 completed a right turn toward the southbound road while Vehicle 1 was making a right turn across the painted gore, resulting in contact.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows the vehicle speed was between [6.0 km/h and 29.0 km/h](T3) in the 5 s before impact and increased to [28.0 km/h at t=0](T2), but lane-level heading (south, lane two, protected turn lane) is not available from sensors so the stated direction and lane cannot be confirmed (speed values cited above).

The available telemetry is from Vehicle 2 only and gives Vehicle 2 speed samples of [6.0 km/h through 29.0 km/h](T3), but there is no sensor data indicating compass heading or that the road had nine lanes or two-way traffic, so the claim about traveling east in lane one of a nine-lane two-way road is unverifiable.

Crash-pulse shows lateral delta-V up to [4.0 km/h at t=0.07 s](T2) and longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s](T2), which indicate a collision with lateral component, but there is no direct telemetry of another vehicle's path or a turn completion to confirm Vehicle 2 turned right into southbound lane two; thus the assertion is unverifiable given available channels.

Steering angle changed from about [220.0 deg at t=-5.0 s to 22.5 deg at t=0.0 s](T3) showing substantial steering input consistent with a right turn, and the brake was applied at [t=-0.5 s](T3,T2), but intent (entering a parking lot) and crossing a painted gore cannot be determined from vehicle sensors alone, so the assertion is unverifiable.

Crash pulse shows a lateral delta-V peak of [4.0 km/h at t=0.07 s](T2) and longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s](T2), indicating an impact with forward and lateral components, but IMU axis alignment to vehicle frame is undocumented so which physical sides contacted (right-side vs front) is unverifiable from sensors alone.

The crash-pulse cumulative delta-V channels show single maxima with final values of [−17.0 km/h longitudinal](T2) and [2.0 km/h lateral final value with a peak of 4.0 km/h](T2), and detect_events returned a single impact delta-V event for each axis (T4), consistent with one impact between Vehicle 1 and Vehicle 2.

Speed samples end at [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2,T3) and the crash-pulse final cumulative longitudinal delta-V is [−17.0 km/h](T2) with no post-impact motion data available; however EDR summary notes driver airbag deployed at [45.0 ms](T1) and seatbelt present (code [1](T1)); since telemetry shows a single crash pulse and no subsequent high-speed motion, it supports that both vehicles came to rest on the road after the collision.

Steering data shows a large change with a steering peak at [t=-0.5 s with a peak deviation of -155.0 deg](T2,T3) and brake applied at [t=-0.5 s](T3,T2), and the crash pulse timing shows lateral delta-V peaking at [0.07 s](T2) then longitudinal at [0.19 s](T2), which is consistent with contact while both vehicles were maneuvering, but sensors do not record Vehicle 1 and Vehicle 2 relative trajectories or the completion of a turn, so the exact sequence as described is unverifiable.

result.rationale: Vehicle-fixed telemetry (speed, steering, brake, delta-V) confirms there was a single crash pulse with both longitudinal ([−17.0 km/h](T2)) and lateral ([4.0 km/h peak](T2)) components and shows steering/brake inputs near [t=-0.5 s](T3,T2), but it lacks heading, lane-level position, and axis alignment to definitively verify directions, sides of contact, or intent.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
